# UC-13.5.5 · Log-to-Trace Correlation Coverage, Trace-ID Propagation Fidelity, and Cross-Telemetry Linkability Health

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Availability &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We check that your error logs still carry the same secret thread ID as your traces so engineers can jump from a scary line straight into the full story in Splunk APM instead of guessing times across systems.*

---

## Description

Measures and detects degradation in cross-telemetry linkability: percent of log events per service emitting trace_id, span_id, and service.name, proxy success of W3C traceparent and tracestate plus B3 header propagation into log-accessible fields, warn-level correlation coverage for Splunk APM Related Content, RUM beacon trace context alignment, and coarse span-versus-log join imbalance using index=apm sourcetype=apm:traces against index=app_logs and index=otel_logs. Surfaces OTel collector and Smart Agent instrumentation gaps, MDC propagation failures, and broken trace-to-log connector configuration before engineers revert to manual timestamp correlation.

## Value

Platform and reliability leaders gain defensible evidence when dashboards still show traces yet on-call cannot pivot from error logs into those traces, which lengthens incidents and erodes trust in Splunk APM. Developer-experience teams see which services need logging bridge work versus gateway fixes, finance partners see when observability debt is structural rather than a transient blip, and auditors receive lookup-backed SLO narratives instead of anecdotal complaints about missing Related Content.

## Implementation

Land correlated app logs, OTel logs, APM traces, and RUM beacons; normalize service.name, trace_id, and W3C headers; maintain log_correlation_slo_inventory.csv; save observability_uc_13_5_5_log_trace_linkability_health every 15m with earliest=-4h@h; alert on critical/high; route via owner_team; validate Related Content pivots.

## Evidence

Saved search observability_uc_13_5_5_log_trace_linkability_health on a fifteen-minute schedule; exported CSV joined to log_correlation_slo_inventory.csv; dashboard drilldowns to Splunk APM Related Content, index=app_logs, index=otel_logs, and index=apm traces archived weekly to a restricted observability_evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab namespace, disable MDC trace injection for a canary service while spans continue, confirm observability_uc_13_5_5_log_trace_linkability_health returns critical or high within two fifteen-minute buckets with non-null recommended_action and owner_team after lookup join.

### Negative scenario

After restoring logging context bridges and validating traceparent through ingress, when warn_trace_coverage_pct meets lookup min_warn_trace_cov for sixty minutes and header_propagation_proxy stabilizes, confirm severities return to low or medium and the alert wrapper emits zero rows filtering to critical and high only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability platform engineers, service owners, and developer-experience leads who are accountable for Splunk APM, Splunk Observability Cloud, Splunk Enterprise log pipelines, and OpenTelemetry Collector fleets that land application logs beside distributed traces. This use case is deliberately about log-to-trace linkability and cross-telemetry pivot health—not structural span parent resolution (UC-13.5.3), not sampling yield mathematics (UC-13.3.7), and not live dependency graph discovery (UC-13.5.4). When correlation coverage collapses, Splunk APM Related Content stops offering trustworthy trace pivots from error lines, engineers fall back to wall-clock guessing across services, and incident reviews lose the tamper-resistant narrative that ties a customer-impacting log fingerprint to an exact trace_id.

Contract you are enforcing: every WARN or ERROR class application log line that represents a user-visible or money-moving failure path should carry a non-zero trace_id and span_id (or equivalent hex identifiers) aligned with W3C Trace Context, and ingress or sidecar layers should preserve traceparent, tracestate, and pragmatic B3 single or multi headers so outbound logs and spans share one trace identifier. OpenTelemetry bridges logs to traces through resource attributes such as service.name, service.version, deployment.environment, and instrumentation scope metadata in otel.scope.name; auto-instrumentation agents populate these fields when configured, while hand-rolled log appenders must mirror the same identifiers into JSON or key-value formats Splunk indexes as first-class fields.

Inventory and lookup discipline: maintain log_correlation_slo_inventory.csv keyed on lowercase service and deployment_environment with columns target_log_trace_pct, min_warn_trace_cov, min_header_cov, owner_team, notes_on_framework, and related_content_expectation. The file is the declarative SLO surface this search joins—do not hard-code ninety percent targets inside SPL when tier-two batch services legitimately differ. CMDB or service catalog exports should align service keys with APM service names; casing drift between checkout-api and Checkout_API silently fractures joins and makes coverage look worse than reality.

RBAC and privacy: trace_id values are correlation keys, not secrets, yet paired with raw log payloads they can reconstruct individual journeys. Restrict indexes holding full payloads to engineering roles with job need, segregate production from non-production using deployment.environment, and hash end-user identifiers before paste into public chat when this search surfaces exemplar rows during incidents. Legal review should confirm retention on index=app_logs, index=otel_logs, and index=apm matches observability policy and that Splunk Observability Cloud exports mirrored into Enterprise obey data residency clauses.

Technical vocabulary for reviewers: MDC in Java, mapped diagnostic context in .NET, and structlog contextvars in Python are the local carriers that must receive the active OpenTelemetry span context before the logging pipeline emits a line. If the logger clears context too early on asynchronous continuations, you will see intermittent coverage that looks like parser bugs but is actually thread-pool boundary loss. gRPC and HTTP clients must use inject calls from the OpenTelemetry propagator API; reverse proxies and API gateways must forward traceparent without stripping unknown headers when security policies allow. Kafka, RabbitMQ, and Amazon SQS publishers should copy trace context into message headers agreed with consumers; a schema-registry upgrade that drops optional headers is a classic regression that shows up as header_propagation_proxy drops without any single deploy in application code.

Splunk product touchpoints: Splunk APM Related Content surfaces associated logs and traces when identifiers and service dimensions align; degraded log_trace_coverage_pct directly erodes that experience even if trace volume looks healthy. The logs-to-traces connector paths in OpenTelemetry Collector, combined with Splunk HTTP Event Collector or Observability Cloud ingest, must preserve trace_id on OTLP log records. When Smart Agent or Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector versions drift across clusters, expect per-cluster coverage variance until you normalize versions via fleet management.

Risk briefing: short deploy windows where logging restarts before tracing will show a thin coverage dip; use streamstats rollups to require sustained deviation before paging. Canary namespaces that intentionally run without trace export for cost experiments must carry lookup annotations so they do not poison fleet dashboards. Third-party SaaS callbacks may emit logs you index without any trace context by contract; model them as excluded rows or separate service keys rather than chasing impossible SLOs.

Differentiation recap: log field presence for trace_id and span_id, header fidelity for traceparent, tracestate, and B3, warn-level correlation emphasis for Splunk APM pivot ergonomics, OTel logs receiver parity, RUM beacon trace context as a fourth multisearch arm, and Performance datamodel tstats as a coarse workload anchor—not orphan ratios, not sampling bias charts, not service map adjacency tables.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane one lands developer-facing application logs in index=app_logs with sourcetypes your TA defines for JSON, log4j, or structured microservice lines. Required extractions: trace_id, span_id, traceparent, tracestate, any B3 fields your mesh emits, service.name, service.version, deployment.environment, log_level, and http route or operation identifiers when present. Normalize hexadecimal trace identifiers to lowercase at index-time to reduce join friction against index=apm sourcetype=apm:traces. Preserve otel.scope.name when the Splunk OTel Collector attaches it to bridged logs.

Lane two lands OpenTelemetry Protocol logs from the collector filelog or OTLP receivers into index=otel_logs. Ensure the receiver copies trace context from the LogRecord into indexed fields, not only into nested JSON blobs you cannot search efficiently. When using the Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector, validate that processors do not strip trace context during attribute limit drops; attribute truncation can remove trace_id while leaving severity intact, which is worse for investigations than dropping the whole event.

Lane three lands Splunk APM trace spans in index=apm with sourcetype=apm:traces (adjust if your tenant maps mirrored OTLP spans to a parallel sourcetype, but keep one canonical arm for span volume and distinct trace_id counts). Include service.name, span_id, parent span linkage fields your pipeline already emits, deployment.environment, service.version, and otel.scope.name for instrumentation diversity tracking.

Lane four lands real user monitoring beacons in index=rum, index=browser_rum, or a curated index=web partition with sourcetype=sfrum or vendor equivalents. Extract traceId or trace_id when the browser SDK participates in a trace with backend spans. RUM coverage is not the primary SLO, yet absence of trace context in RUM while backend logs lose trace_id often indicates the same gateway stripping headers at the edge.

HTTP Event Collector tokens for each lane require rate-limit monitoring; burst 429 responses can create artificial coverage dips. Clock skew between log agents and trace exporters biases fifteen-minute buckets; maintain NTP discipline and consider five-minute buckets only when skew is provably below one second across the fleet.

props.conf and transforms.conf should FIELDALIAS dotted OTel resource attributes into service.name style fields consumed by this search. Maintain a small macro that expands index macros so multi-tenant teams can override index=app_logs without editing the saved search body.

Pre-save validation: pick a known trace_id from Splunk APM, query index=app_logs for that trace_id within a tight window, and confirm non-zero events. Repeat for index=otel_logs. If APM shows a trace while logs return nothing, Related Content will feel broken even when raw tailing works—fix field extraction before tuning thresholds.

Dashboards: publish a coverage heatmap by service and environment with drilldowns opening Splunk APM trace views and the Related Content panel documentation your organization standardizes.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_5_5_log_trace_linkability_health with a fifteen-minute schedule in steady production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after volume guards on log_events or span_rows. Throttle duplicate service, deployment_environment, and service_version tuples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from high to critical. Include recommended_action, owner_team, gap_pct, and warn_trace_coverage_pct in pager narratives so responders open MDC runbooks, gateway header allow lists, OTel auto-instrumentation guides, and Splunk APM Related Content troubleshooting without re-deriving the math.

Pipeline narrative unique to linkability: multisearch fans four heterogeneous telemetry lanes—application logs with trace identifiers, OTel collector logs index, Splunk APM traces, and RUM beacons—so a silent lane cannot hide a genuine regression. coalesce() ladders normalize service.name versus flat service field naming. eval computes log_trace_coverage_pct, warn_trace_coverage_pct, and header_propagation_proxy ratios; where removes low-volume noise rows that would false-positive in quiet tiers. eventstats captures per-environment median coverage for contextual drift. streamstats supplies short rolling averages to dampen single-bucket flicker. join to log_correlation_slo_inventory.csv enforces declared targets per service and environment. A second join runs tstats against the Splunk CIM Performance datamodel as a coarse anchor tying service keys to Performance.process host activity counts—not to assert causality, but to prove the search executed in tenants with accelerated Performance summaries. case() encodes severity emphasizing warn-level trace omissions that break Splunk APM Related Content pivots before all-log coverage dips, because executives read incidents through error lines first.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.5 log-trace linkability multisearch app_logs otel_logs apm rum Tunables earliest=-4h@h bucket=15m")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=app_logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,service,"unknown_svc"))))
      | eval deployment_environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_env")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,"unset_version")
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,traceId,"")
      | eval span_id=coalesce(span_id,spanId,"")
      | eval log_valid_trace=if(len(trace_id)>=16 AND NOT match(lower(trace_id),"^0+$"),1,0)
      | eval lev=lower(toString(coalesce(log_level,level,severity,priority,"info")))
      | eval warn_error_row=if(match(lev,"warn|warning|error|err|fatal|critical"),1,0)
      | eval warn_with_trace=if(warn_error_row==1 AND log_valid_trace==1,1,0)
      | eval hdr_tp=lower(toString(coalesce(traceparent,'traceparent',http_traceparent,"")))
      | eval header_w3c_ok=if(len(hdr_tp)>=20 AND match(hdr_tp,"^[0-9a-f]{2}-[0-9a-f]{32}-[0-9a-f]{16}-[0-9a-f]{2}$"),1,0)
      | eval b3_single=toString(coalesce(b3,b3_single,b3_traceid,""))
      | eval b3_hdr=toString(coalesce(b3_multi_header,x_b3_traceid,""))
      | eval header_legacy_b3=if(len(b3_single)>=16 OR len(b3_hdr)>0,1,0)
      | eval tracestate_val=toString(coalesce(tracestate,'tracestate',http_tracestate,""))
      | eval tracestate_ok=if(len(tracestate_val)>0,1,0)
      | eval header_ok=if(header_w3c_ok==1 OR header_legacy_b3==1 OR tracestate_ok==1,1,0)
      | bin _time span=15m
      | stats count AS log_events sum(log_valid_trace) AS logs_with_trace sum(warn_error_row) AS warn_err_logs sum(warn_with_trace) AS warn_logs_with_trace sum(header_ok) AS log_rows_with_headers BY _time service deployment_environment service_version
      | eval lane="app_logs_trace_ctx"
      | eval span_rows=0
      | eval distinct_traces=0
      | eval span_with_trace_id=0
      | eval otel_scope_dc=0
      | eval rum_hits=0
      | eval rum_with_trace=0 ]
    [ search index=otel_logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',resource_attrs_service_name,service,"unknown_svc"))))
      | eval deployment_environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_env")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,"unset_version")
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,"")
      | eval otel_scope_name=coalesce('otel.scope.name',otel_scope_name,"unset_otel_scope")
      | eval log_valid_trace=if(len(trace_id)>=16 AND NOT match(lower(trace_id),"^0+$"),1,0)
      | eval warn_error_row=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce(severity_text,severity,""))),"warn|error|fatal") OR tonumber(coalesce(severityNumber,0),10)>=17,1,0)
      | eval warn_with_trace=if(warn_error_row==1 AND log_valid_trace==1,1,0)
      | eval hdr_tp=lower(toString(coalesce(traceparent,'traceparent',"")))
      | eval header_w3c_ok=if(len(hdr_tp)>=20 AND match(hdr_tp,"^[0-9a-f]{2}-[0-9a-f]{32}-[0-9a-f]{16}-[0-9a-f]{2}$"),1,0)
      | eval tracestate_val=toString(coalesce(tracestate,'tracestate',""))
      | eval tracestate_ok=if(len(tracestate_val)>0,1,0)
      | eval b3_single=toString(coalesce(b3,b3_single,""))
      | eval header_legacy_b3=if(len(b3_single)>=16,1,0)
      | eval header_ok=if(header_w3c_ok==1 OR tracestate_ok==1 OR header_legacy_b3==1,1,0)
      | bin _time span=15m
      | stats count AS log_events sum(log_valid_trace) AS logs_with_trace sum(warn_error_row) AS warn_err_logs sum(warn_with_trace) AS warn_logs_with_trace sum(header_ok) AS log_rows_with_headers dc(otel_scope_name) AS otel_scope_dc BY _time service deployment_environment service_version
      | eval lane="otel_logs_receiver_lane"
      | eval span_rows=0
      | eval distinct_traces=0
      | eval span_with_trace_id=0
      | eval rum_hits=0
      | eval rum_with_trace=0 ]
    [ search index=apm sourcetype=apm:traces earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,"unknown_svc"))))
      | eval deployment_environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_env")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,"unset_version")
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,"")
      | eval span_id=coalesce(span_id,"")
      | eval otel_scope_name=coalesce('otel.scope.name',otel_instrumentation_scope_name,"apm_scope")
      | bin _time span=15m
      | stats count AS span_rows dc(trace_id) AS distinct_traces sum(eval(if(len(trace_id)>=16,1,0))) AS span_with_trace_id dc(otel_scope_name) AS otel_scope_dc BY _time service deployment_environment service_version
      | eval log_events=0
      | eval logs_with_trace=0
      | eval warn_err_logs=0
      | eval warn_logs_with_trace=0
      | eval log_rows_with_headers=0
      | eval lane="apm_traces_lane"
      | eval rum_hits=0
      | eval rum_with_trace=0 ]
    [ search (index=rum OR index=browser_rum OR index=web) earliest=-4h@h latest=now (sourcetype=sfrum OR sourcetype=rum_beacon OR sourcetype=rum* OR sf_service=*)
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',sf_service,app_id,"unknown_svc"))))
      | eval deployment_environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',environment,"unknown_env")
      | eval service_version=coalesce(app_version,'service.version',"unset_version")
      | eval tid=coalesce(traceId,trace_id,"")
      | eval rum_with_trace=if(len(tid)>=16 AND NOT match(lower(tid),"^0+$"),1,0)
      | bin _time span=15m
      | stats count AS rum_hits sum(rum_with_trace) AS rum_with_trace BY _time service deployment_environment service_version
      | eval log_events=0
      | eval logs_with_trace=0
      | eval warn_err_logs=0
      | eval warn_logs_with_trace=0
      | eval log_rows_with_headers=0
      | eval span_rows=0
      | eval distinct_traces=0
      | eval span_with_trace_id=0
      | eval otel_scope_dc=0
      | eval lane="rum_beacon_lane" ]
| eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,"unknown_svc"))))
| eval deployment_environment=coalesce(deployment_environment,"unknown_env")
| stats sum(log_events) AS log_events sum(logs_with_trace) AS logs_with_trace sum(warn_err_logs) AS warn_err_logs sum(warn_logs_with_trace) AS warn_logs_with_trace sum(log_rows_with_headers) AS log_rows_with_headers sum(span_rows) AS span_rows sum(distinct_traces) AS distinct_trace_samples sum(span_with_trace_id) AS span_tid_rows sum(rum_hits) AS rum_hits sum(rum_with_trace) AS rum_with_trace max(otel_scope_dc) AS otel_scope_dc BY _time service deployment_environment service_version
| eval log_trace_coverage_pct=if(log_events>0, round(100*logs_with_trace/log_events,2), null())
| eval warn_trace_coverage_pct=if(warn_err_logs>0, round(100*warn_logs_with_trace/warn_err_logs,2), null())
| eval header_propagation_proxy=if(log_events>0, round(100*log_rows_with_headers/log_events,2), null())
| where log_events>=20 OR span_rows>=50 OR rum_hits>=5
| join type=left max=0 service deployment_environment
    [| inputlookup log_correlation_slo_inventory.csv
     | eval service=lower(trim(toString(service)))
     | eval deployment_environment=coalesce(env,deployment_environment,environment)
     | fields service deployment_environment target_log_trace_pct min_warn_trace_cov min_header_cov owner_team ]
| join type=left max=0 service
    [| tstats summariesonly=t count AS perf_rows FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.process
     | rename Performance.process AS service
     | eval service=lower(trim(toString(service))) ]
| eval target_log_trace_pct=tonumber(coalesce(target_log_trace_pct,90),10)
| eval min_warn_trace_cov=tonumber(coalesce(min_warn_trace_cov,80),10)
| eval min_header_cov=tonumber(coalesce(min_header_cov,70),10)
| eval gap_pct=if(isnotnull(log_trace_coverage_pct), round(target_log_trace_pct-log_trace_coverage_pct,2), null())
| eval orphan_link_ratio=if(distinct_trace_samples>0 AND logs_with_trace>0, round(max(0,1-(logs_with_trace/distinct_trace_samples)),4), null())
| eventstats median(log_trace_coverage_pct) AS env_median_log_cov BY deployment_environment
| streamstats window=4 current=t global=f avg(log_trace_coverage_pct) AS log_cov_roll BY service deployment_environment service_version
| eval related_content_risk=if(isnotnull(warn_trace_coverage_pct) AND warn_trace_coverage_pct<min_warn_trace_cov AND warn_err_logs>=15,1,0)
| eval severity=case(related_content_risk==1 AND log_events>=40,"critical",log_trace_coverage_pct<(0.85*target_log_trace_pct) AND log_events>=80,"high",header_propagation_proxy<min_header_cov AND log_events>=60,"high",isnotnull(orphan_link_ratio) AND orphan_link_ratio>0.35 AND span_rows>=100,"medium",true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","restore_mdc_otel_logging_context_validate_splunk_apm_related_content_trace_pivots",severity=="high" AND header_propagation_proxy<min_header_cov,"repair_gateway_mesh_w3c_traceparent_tracestate_and_b3_injection",severity=="high","expand_otel_zero_code_auto_instrumentation_and_apm_trace_to_log_connector",true(),"tune_log_correlation_slo_inventory_and_otel_scope_diversity")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned_observability")
| table _time service deployment_environment service_version log_trace_coverage_pct warn_trace_coverage_pct header_propagation_proxy gap_pct orphan_link_ratio rum_with_trace span_rows otel_scope_dc perf_rows severity recommended_action owner_team
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_5_5_log_trace_linkability_health_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_5_5_log_trace_linkability_health | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time exceeding internal SLOs, materialize each lane into fifteen-minute summary indexes keyed on service, deployment_environment, and service_version, widen operational alerts to earliest=-90m@m on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. For very high-cardinality RUM indexes, restrict the RUM arm with a summary index fed from a lightweight scheduled search.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins with a single trace_id copied from Splunk APM or Observability Cloud Trace Analyzer. Within the same fifteen-minute wall clock, run narrow searches across index=app_logs and index=otel_logs; counts should be positive for instrumented services. If logs exist without trace_id while spans show the same operation, coverage math in this UC is correctly flagging a logging bridge gap.

Validate header propagation by capturing ingress access logs alongside application logs during a canary; traceparent should appear on a statistically representative fraction of inbound requests matching header_propagation_proxy. Loss concentrated at one gateway cluster implicates mesh config, not microservice log4j.

Validate lookup governance by running inputlookup log_correlation_slo_inventory.csv alone and confirming owner_team and targets for tier-zero services. Validate tstats arm by confirming the Performance datamodel accelerates in the tenant; if summariesonly=t returns null rows, remove acceleration gating or switch the join to a simple metadata lookup until acceleration is built.

Synthetic negative test: in non-production, temporarily disable MDC injection for a canary pod, confirm severity rises within two buckets, then re-enable and confirm decay. Positive calm test: on a steady day without collector changes, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for sixty consecutive runs when thresholds are calibrated.

Runbook linkage: tie validation outcomes to OpenTelemetry logging bridge documentation, W3C Trace Context propagation checklists, Splunk APM Related Content prerequisites, and internal onboarding guides for Splunk OTel Collector versions pinned per cluster.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Java Log4j2 AsyncAppender clears MDC before trace_id prints: switch to synchronous appenders for diagnostic windows, adopt OpenTelemetry Log4j2 context bridge, or inject trace markers via the API that attaches span context to log events.

Case 2 — .NET ILogger scopes lose Activity context on thread-pool hops: enable OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting instrumentation, verify Activity.Current propagation into custom Task.Run work, and validate Serilog enrichers read Activity trace identifiers.

Case 3 — Python structlog binds contextvars but uvicorn worker recycling drops context: align instrumentation packages, upgrade opentelemetry-instrumentation-asgi, and confirm gunicorn worker class supports context propagation.

Case 4 — Envoy or NGINX ingress strips trace headers by default: add explicit traceparent and tracestate forward rules, allow B3 single header for legacy meshes, and regression-test with curl loops carrying synthetic traceparent values.

Case 5 — Istio telemetry v2 configuration omits trace headers on outbound cluster egress: patch VirtualService http headers, document allowed vendor extensions, and replay Kafka producer traces after fixing sidecar ordering.

Case 6 — OpenTelemetry Collector attributes processor truncates trace_id on logs but not spans: raise attribute limits, split noisy attributes into dedicated pipelines, and validate drops via internal otelcol_exporter_sent metric lanes.

Case 7 — Splunk HEC ACK lag makes logs arrive after spans in the same bucket: widen bucket span modestly for alerting only, or use summary indexing with aligned timestamps; do not mute the UC without acknowledging Related Content lag.

Case 8 — RUM beacon uses a different service key than APM service.name: normalize sf_service and service.name via lookup aliases so backend and browser lanes merge; otherwise rum_with_trace looks orphaned.

Case 9 — Log sampling at the collector drops WARN lines with trace_id while INFO lines remain: rebalance tail sampling or disable aggressive log sampling on severity tiers tied to SLO paths.

Case 10 — Dual-write to Splunk Cloud and Enterprise duplicates logs and dilutes coverage ratios: deduplicate on ingest keys or split indexes per environment before trusting gap_pct week over week.

Case 11 — Smart Agent auto-instrumentation version skew across VMs misses Flask hooks on a subset of hosts: standardize splunk_apm_smart_agent packages, roll forward uniformly, and verify otel.scope.name diversity returns.

Case 12 — Related Content UI shows traces but log panel empty despite healthy coverage metrics: validate Splunk APM trace-to-log integration configuration, index allow lists for pivot queries, and role-based search filters that hide app_logs from APM service accounts.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists four lanes covering app logs, OTel logs, APM traces, and RUM; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, and tstats appear; final table lists sixteen analyst-visible columns including severity, recommended_action, and owner_team for accountable routing.

Teaching notes for platform auditors: archive weekly CSV exports of critical and high severity rows to a restricted observability_evidence index with change ticket references when lookup targets move. When executives ask why correlation matters, answer with mean time to pivot: Related Content collapses minutes of manual join work into one click when trace_id is present on the error line.

Extended narrative on Splunk APM integration: the trace-to-log feature relies on consistent service dimensions and trace identifiers flowing into both signal types. If service.version differs between logs and spans because deploy tags are missing on one lane, pivots may still fail despite numerically high coverage; enforce service.version stamping in CI for containers and VMs alike.

Extended narrative on OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation: zero-code agents reduce classloader mistakes yet introduce version coupling between agent and application runtime. Maintain a compatibility matrix in the lookup notes column and page service owners when agent bumps trail security patches.

Extended narrative on W3C tracestate: vendor-specific entries carry sampling hints and auxiliary flags; stripping tracestate while preserving traceparent can still break tail sampling coordination across vendors. Document which keys your mesh preserves.

Extended narrative on B3: single-header B3 encoding remains common in Spring Cloud and Zipkin bridges; multisearch counts header_legacy_b3 style hints when single or multi header fields appear in extracted columns.

Capacity planning: multisearch fanout scales with cardinality of service by environment; summary indexing is mandatory beyond roughly five thousand active keys.

Stakeholder communication: product owners care about error triage speed; translate severity rows into probable minutes lost per incident using internal incident postmortems where correlation was missing.

Security teams should review who can edit log_correlation_slo_inventory.csv in GitOps; unauthorized merges can hide true regressions behind inflated targets.

FinOps partners should understand that raising coverage often increases indexed field cardinality slightly; negotiate index budgets alongside SLO improvements.

Post-incident reviews should ask whether the regression began at ingress headers, logging MDC, OTel collector processors, or Splunk field extraction order.

For multi-region estates, document which clusters share collectors; imbalanced shards can bias coverage per region even when global traffic looks steady.

IPv6-only ingress sometimes normalizes headers differently than IPv4 paths; validate traceparent on both stacks during gateway upgrades.

Service meshes may inject baggage that explodes attribute cardinality; trim before log export to keep Splunk index sizes predictable.

Synthetic chaos experiments that strip trace headers should carry labeled namespaces so this UC can suppress known drill windows.

Vendor support bundles should include redacted multisearch outputs, exemplar trace_id values, and Related Content screenshots for the same minute bucket.

Continuous improvement: when OpenTelemetry releases new logging semantic conventions, revalidate FIELDALIAS maps and update the OTel logs arm field list.

Long-term architecture: evaluate separate collector pools for high-cardinality browser RUM export versus stable service logs to keep queue latency predictable.

Human judgment still validates automated severity; this search guides investigations during customer-impacting outages without replacing incident commander context.


Teaching notes for platform auditors: archive weekly CSV exports of critical and high severity rows to a restricted observability_evidence index with change ticket references when lookup targets move. ## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.5 log-trace linkability multisearch app_logs otel_logs apm rum Tunables earliest=-4h@h bucket=15m")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=app_logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,service,"unknown_svc"))))
      | eval deployment_environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_env")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,"unset_version")
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,traceId,"")
      | eval span_id=coalesce(span_id,spanId,"")
      | eval log_valid_trace=if(len(trace_id)>=16 AND NOT match(lower(trace_id),"^0+$"),1,0)
      | eval lev=lower(toString(coalesce(log_level,level,severity,priority,"info")))
      | eval warn_error_row=if(match(lev,"warn|warning|error|err|fatal|critical"),1,0)
      | eval warn_with_trace=if(warn_error_row==1 AND log_valid_trace==1,1,0)
      | eval hdr_tp=lower(toString(coalesce(traceparent,'traceparent',http_traceparent,"")))
      | eval header_w3c_ok=if(len(hdr_tp)>=20 AND match(hdr_tp,"^[0-9a-f]{2}-[0-9a-f]{32}-[0-9a-f]{16}-[0-9a-f]{2}$"),1,0)
      | eval b3_single=toString(coalesce(b3,b3_single,b3_traceid,""))
      | eval b3_hdr=toString(coalesce(b3_multi_header,x_b3_traceid,""))
      | eval header_legacy_b3=if(len(b3_single)>=16 OR len(b3_hdr)>0,1,0)
      | eval tracestate_val=toString(coalesce(tracestate,'tracestate',http_tracestate,""))
      | eval tracestate_ok=if(len(tracestate_val)>0,1,0)
      | eval header_ok=if(header_w3c_ok==1 OR header_legacy_b3==1 OR tracestate_ok==1,1,0)
      | bin _time span=15m
      | stats count AS log_events sum(log_valid_trace) AS logs_with_trace sum(warn_error_row) AS warn_err_logs sum(warn_with_trace) AS warn_logs_with_trace sum(header_ok) AS log_rows_with_headers BY _time service deployment_environment service_version
      | eval lane="app_logs_trace_ctx"
      | eval span_rows=0
      | eval distinct_traces=0
      | eval span_with_trace_id=0
      | eval otel_scope_dc=0
      | eval rum_hits=0
      | eval rum_with_trace=0 ]
    [ search index=otel_logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',resource_attrs_service_name,service,"unknown_svc"))))
      | eval deployment_environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_env")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,"unset_version")
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,"")
      | eval otel_scope_name=coalesce('otel.scope.name',otel_scope_name,"unset_otel_scope")
      | eval log_valid_trace=if(len(trace_id)>=16 AND NOT match(lower(trace_id),"^0+$"),1,0)
      | eval warn_error_row=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce(severity_text,severity,""))),"warn|error|fatal") OR tonumber(coalesce(severityNumber,0),10)>=17,1,0)
      | eval warn_with_trace=if(warn_error_row==1 AND log_valid_trace==1,1,0)
      | eval hdr_tp=lower(toString(coalesce(traceparent,'traceparent',"")))
      | eval header_w3c_ok=if(len(hdr_tp)>=20 AND match(hdr_tp,"^[0-9a-f]{2}-[0-9a-f]{32}-[0-9a-f]{16}-[0-9a-f]{2}$"),1,0)
      | eval tracestate_val=toString(coalesce(tracestate,'tracestate',""))
      | eval tracestate_ok=if(len(tracestate_val)>0,1,0)
      | eval b3_single=toString(coalesce(b3,b3_single,""))
      | eval header_legacy_b3=if(len(b3_single)>=16,1,0)
      | eval header_ok=if(header_w3c_ok==1 OR tracestate_ok==1 OR header_legacy_b3==1,1,0)
      | bin _time span=15m
      | stats count AS log_events sum(log_valid_trace) AS logs_with_trace sum(warn_error_row) AS warn_err_logs sum(warn_with_trace) AS warn_logs_with_trace sum(header_ok) AS log_rows_with_headers dc(otel_scope_name) AS otel_scope_dc BY _time service deployment_environment service_version
      | eval lane="otel_logs_receiver_lane"
      | eval span_rows=0
      | eval distinct_traces=0
      | eval span_with_trace_id=0
      | eval rum_hits=0
      | eval rum_with_trace=0 ]
    [ search index=apm sourcetype=apm:traces earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,"unknown_svc"))))
      | eval deployment_environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_env")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,"unset_version")
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,"")
      | eval span_id=coalesce(span_id,"")
      | eval otel_scope_name=coalesce('otel.scope.name',otel_instrumentation_scope_name,"apm_scope")
      | bin _time span=15m
      | stats count AS span_rows dc(trace_id) AS distinct_traces sum(eval(if(len(trace_id)>=16,1,0))) AS span_with_trace_id dc(otel_scope_name) AS otel_scope_dc BY _time service deployment_environment service_version
      | eval log_events=0
      | eval logs_with_trace=0
      | eval warn_err_logs=0
      | eval warn_logs_with_trace=0
      | eval log_rows_with_headers=0
      | eval lane="apm_traces_lane"
      | eval rum_hits=0
      | eval rum_with_trace=0 ]
    [ search (index=rum OR index=browser_rum OR index=web) earliest=-4h@h latest=now (sourcetype=sfrum OR sourcetype=rum_beacon OR sourcetype=rum* OR sf_service=*)
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',sf_service,app_id,"unknown_svc"))))
      | eval deployment_environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',environment,"unknown_env")
      | eval service_version=coalesce(app_version,'service.version',"unset_version")
      | eval tid=coalesce(traceId,trace_id,"")
      | eval rum_with_trace=if(len(tid)>=16 AND NOT match(lower(tid),"^0+$"),1,0)
      | bin _time span=15m
      | stats count AS rum_hits sum(rum_with_trace) AS rum_with_trace BY _time service deployment_environment service_version
      | eval log_events=0
      | eval logs_with_trace=0
      | eval warn_err_logs=0
      | eval warn_logs_with_trace=0
      | eval log_rows_with_headers=0
      | eval span_rows=0
      | eval distinct_traces=0
      | eval span_with_trace_id=0
      | eval otel_scope_dc=0
      | eval lane="rum_beacon_lane" ]
| eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,"unknown_svc"))))
| eval deployment_environment=coalesce(deployment_environment,"unknown_env")
| stats sum(log_events) AS log_events sum(logs_with_trace) AS logs_with_trace sum(warn_err_logs) AS warn_err_logs sum(warn_logs_with_trace) AS warn_logs_with_trace sum(log_rows_with_headers) AS log_rows_with_headers sum(span_rows) AS span_rows sum(distinct_traces) AS distinct_trace_samples sum(span_with_trace_id) AS span_tid_rows sum(rum_hits) AS rum_hits sum(rum_with_trace) AS rum_with_trace max(otel_scope_dc) AS otel_scope_dc BY _time service deployment_environment service_version
| eval log_trace_coverage_pct=if(log_events>0, round(100*logs_with_trace/log_events,2), null())
| eval warn_trace_coverage_pct=if(warn_err_logs>0, round(100*warn_logs_with_trace/warn_err_logs,2), null())
| eval header_propagation_proxy=if(log_events>0, round(100*log_rows_with_headers/log_events,2), null())
| where log_events>=20 OR span_rows>=50 OR rum_hits>=5
| join type=left max=0 service deployment_environment
    [| inputlookup log_correlation_slo_inventory.csv
     | eval service=lower(trim(toString(service)))
     | eval deployment_environment=coalesce(env,deployment_environment,environment)
     | fields service deployment_environment target_log_trace_pct min_warn_trace_cov min_header_cov owner_team ]
| join type=left max=0 service
    [| tstats summariesonly=t count AS perf_rows FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.process
     | rename Performance.process AS service
     | eval service=lower(trim(toString(service))) ]
| eval target_log_trace_pct=tonumber(coalesce(target_log_trace_pct,90),10)
| eval min_warn_trace_cov=tonumber(coalesce(min_warn_trace_cov,80),10)
| eval min_header_cov=tonumber(coalesce(min_header_cov,70),10)
| eval gap_pct=if(isnotnull(log_trace_coverage_pct), round(target_log_trace_pct-log_trace_coverage_pct,2), null())
| eval orphan_link_ratio=if(distinct_trace_samples>0 AND logs_with_trace>0, round(max(0,1-(logs_with_trace/distinct_trace_samples)),4), null())
| eventstats median(log_trace_coverage_pct) AS env_median_log_cov BY deployment_environment
| streamstats window=4 current=t global=f avg(log_trace_coverage_pct) AS log_cov_roll BY service deployment_environment service_version
| eval related_content_risk=if(isnotnull(warn_trace_coverage_pct) AND warn_trace_coverage_pct<min_warn_trace_cov AND warn_err_logs>=15,1,0)
| eval severity=case(related_content_risk==1 AND log_events>=40,"critical",log_trace_coverage_pct<(0.85*target_log_trace_pct) AND log_events>=80,"high",header_propagation_proxy<min_header_cov AND log_events>=60,"high",isnotnull(orphan_link_ratio) AND orphan_link_ratio>0.35 AND span_rows>=100,"medium",true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","restore_mdc_otel_logging_context_validate_splunk_apm_related_content_trace_pivots",severity=="high" AND header_propagation_proxy<min_header_cov,"repair_gateway_mesh_w3c_traceparent_tracestate_and_b3_injection",severity=="high","expand_otel_zero_code_auto_instrumentation_and_apm_trace_to_log_connector",true(),"tune_log_correlation_slo_inventory_and_otel_scope_diversity")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned_observability")
| table _time service deployment_environment service_version log_trace_coverage_pct warn_trace_coverage_pct header_propagation_proxy gap_pct orphan_link_ratio rum_with_trace span_rows otel_scope_dc perf_rows severity recommended_action owner_team
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_used count FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance host=* earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.process
| rename Performance.process AS service_proc
| eval service_proc=lower(trim(toString(service_proc)))
| join type=left max=0 service_proc [| inputlookup log_correlation_slo_inventory.csv | eval service_proc=lower(trim(toString(service))) | fields service_proc target_log_trace_pct owner_team]
| table service_proc cpu_load mem_used target_log_trace_pct owner_team
```

## Visualization

Heatmap of log_trace_coverage_pct by service and deployment_environment, line chart of warn_trace_coverage_pct for Related Content risk, table of severity-colored rows with drilldowns to Splunk APM traces and raw app_logs, single-value tiles for critical count, paired timeline of header_propagation_proxy versus gateway change markers.

## Known False Positives

Bootstrap and shutdown log storms often lack active spans by design; require warn-level subsets before paging product teams on whole-log coverage. Infrastructure agents, kernel ring buffers, and CI runner chatter legitimately omit trace context; segregate sourcetypes or service keys in the lookup. Short HEC outages can zero one lane while APM continues; pair with inputstatus dashboards before blaming instrumentation. Canary clusters running reduced instrumentation inflate apparent gaps; annotate deployment_environment rows. Header proxy fields extracted only on a fraction of services make header_propagation_proxy look worse than actual mesh behavior; align FIELDALIAS. RUM ad blockers and privacy browsers strip trace headers; exclude known kiosk fleets. Log sampling policies targeting INFO volume may accidentally undersample WARN lines in some frameworks; validate severity-aware sampling. Duplicate indexing of the same log line across mirrored clusters doubles denominators; deduplicate before SLO reviews. Third-party SaaS webhooks indexed as app_logs without trace contracts will never meet ninety percent targets; mark them out-of-scope in inventory notes. Performance datamodel tstats rows map hosts to processes, not always microservice names; treat perf_rows as supplemental context. Clock skew across regions misaligns fifteen-minute buckets; monitor NTP. Manual ad hoc trace_id searches during incidents can skew rolling streamstats; isolate investigative sessions into separate indexes.

- [Splunk Docs — Use Related Content in Splunk Observability Cloud](https://docs.splunk.com/observability/en/apm/apm-related-content/use-related-content.html)
- [OpenTelemetry — Trace Context in Logs (supplemental guidelines)](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/logs/supplemental-guidelines/)
- [W3C — Trace Context](https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/)
- [OpenTelemetry — Automatic instrumentation](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/concepts/instrumentation/automatic/)
- [Splunk Docs — Splunk APM service map](https://docs.splunk.com/observability/en/apm/apm-service-map/apm-service-map.html)
- [Splunk Lantern — Observability monitoring](https://lantern.splunk.com/Observability)
- [Splunk Docs — Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector](https://docs.splunk.com/observability/en/gdi/opentelemetry/opentelemetry.html)
- [OpenTelemetry — Context propagation](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/concepts/context-propagation/)
